examples: The policy is obliteration, openly acknowledged.
The obliteration of misery will be accomplished by a simple elevation of level.
Any politician who promises ever-rising energy prices, even in a good cause, faces obliteration.
Would such a coalition lead to the gradual obliteration of the Lib Dems' separate identity?
In "No. 9" (1948) we watch Rothko grappling with the near-obliteration of traditional notions of pictorial space.
